dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 6 responding for stroke/CVA at 20th and Broad in Richmond.
Hi, I'm Rachel, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Uh, I think I'm having a stroke. I can't talk properly. Some numbness on my face, too.
Mr. Thompson, can you open your eyes for me?
Can you follow my finger up and down slowly with your eyes, please?
Mr. Thompson, can you tell me, what is your name?
Can you squeeze my fingers in your left hand slowly? Now with your right? Can you press down with both hands while I count to three? One, two, three. One, two, three again.
Mr. Thompson, I'm going to take a quick look at your airway now. Do you mind?
Airway is patent.
Can we take a quick look at his breathing?
Respirations are 16, even on both sides.
SpO2 is 94%, on room air currently.
We're going to take his blood pressure now. Blood pressure is 187 over 123.
Pulse is 75. It's regular and present.
Blood glucose is 116 mg/dL.
Mr. Thompson, can you point out where the pain is most severe? How long has it been there?
The pain started at the back of your neck and shoulders, correct?
Have you had any medical issues in the past, like diabetes?
Yes, I have diabetes. I haven't had a stroke or a migraine before.
Any known allergies?
We're going to use this light now. Can we shine the light in your eyes?
Pupils are equal and reactive.
Mr. Thompson, if I hold up two fingers, can you tell me how many you see?
Let's review arm drift. Raise your hands as high as you can and keep them there. Are you able to hold both arms up?
EKG shows sinus rhythm. We just obtained a 12-lead per protocol. No abnormalities detected on first evaluation.
We're going to start an 18-gauge IV in your left forearm now. How does that sound?
IV line successful. We also started a second one in your right hand about six minutes ago.
Mr. Thompson, can you rate the pain on a scale of 0 to 10 right now?
Mr. Thompson, are you experiencing any unusual sensations, such as numbness?
Yes, there's abnormal sensations from left to right and numbness on the right side of my face.
We're activating the stroke alert protocol based on the VAN scale findings.
Repeat EKG per protocol. Still sinus rhythm, no change.
According to this alert, VCU ED is our best bet. We've placed the patient on a stretcher in a comfortable position, secured with five straps.
RFD helped bring him out. We pivoted him with caution. Everything is secured for transport.
Mr. Thompson is negative for arm drift, facial droop, and slurred speech.
Mr. Thompson's IVs are clear and in place in the left forearm and right hand.
VCU location confirmed. Transport time expected to be 12 minutes. Please go ahead with lights and siren.
Rescue driver has the lane clear. Very few cars. Still 7-1/2 minutes out.
Both IV lines good. No excess resistance, staining.
Mr. Thompson in stable condition. No pain reported.
Mr. Thompson arrived slightly out-of-place with stairs alone. RFD via a stair chair lent support for placing him close. No injury noted in transfer.
Let's move. Paramedic, ensure IV clip stays in place.
Vitals again now. Still 75 pulse, 16 respirations, BP 184 over 132. Glucose unreadable; device error. SpO2 increased to 97%. EKG still sinus rhythm.
Second IV successful again. Flow rate good.
No prior stroke or migraines. But he reported a headache starting on the base of the neck and shoulder, radiating into his head since Friday.
Mr. Thompson is hypersensitive to light.
Next light ahead. All systems nominal. Continue on.
Movement and transit smooth thus far. IV flow is clear.
Language difficulties prevent us from assessing last oral intake.
Patient reported difficulty with specific words. No current facial droop.
Mr. Thompson has non-insulin dependent diabetes in medical history.
EMT will take lead on VCU entry upon arrival. Reattach radio and check in with VCU on channel 9.
Arrival expected in 3 more minutes. IV line counting still progresses satisfactorily.
No evidence of allergies reported.
Stroke alert not accepted by VCU ED upon arrival. Patient taken to regular bed.
Mr. Thompson is currently not reporting any pain.
